milestone: A2 (A-b): in-place COW commit on any container size (256 MiB)
status: Passed (host layout verify + Apple kernel reads file + Apple fsck_apfs clean, incl. allocated-space verification). In-place commit is layout-general.
capability: computeGeneratedLayout derives the free-region start from ipDelta = 3*(chunk_count+1) - kApfsFormatIpBlockCount and caps allocation to chunk 0; the live chunk-info block + bitmap stay at their fixed internal-pool addresses (187/188) for any size. The in-place commit source cap is raised to the A1-certified 256 MiB single-CIB tier. Fixes a latent bug: the old hardcoded 188/187/201 silently produced an inconsistent container above 128 MiB.
host_verify: 256 MiB container (block_count 65536, chunk_count 2, ipDelta 3): COW chain + data at the shifted free region (seed 204, data 211); the fixed chunk-0 bitmap (188) marks blocks 204-211 used; chunk-info (187) chunk-0 free count 32564 -> 32563 (no underflow). 64 MiB output byte-identical (ipDelta 0).

vm_external_gate.environment: qemu/KVM macOS Sequoia recovery; apfs.kext 2332.101.1; 256 MiB container with a file inserted in place (xid 3) attached raw as SATA disk0
method: 2. Apple kernel auto-mounted /Volumes/BIG256; `cat big.txt` = 'content in a 256MB container' (apfs.kext read the file from the 256 MiB container).
3. diskutil unmount; fsck_apfs -n /dev/disk0 -> no warnings, including 'Verifying allocated space' (the fixed-bitmap accounting is correct).
vm_external_gate.apple_fsck_apfs: Checking the checkpoint with transaction ID 5 ... fsroot tree / extent ref tree / volume object map space / allocated space all consistent; The volume BIG256 appears to be OK; The container /dev/disk0 appears to be OK (no warnings).
vm_external_gate.evidence_png: big256-fsck.png (cat big.txt + clean fsck on the 256 MiB container)

gates: clang-format + lizard + cppcheck clean; commit 9feaf75 (all pre-commit hooks passed, no bypass).
next: Free-queue crash-safety, multi-CIB (>256 MiB, multiple chunks/bitmaps), arbitrary real-Apple containers, multi-leaf fstree, fragmented free space.
